disputed_language: {"conditions":["AWS RAM sharing with AWS Organizations is enabled through AWS RAM.","The organization has all features enabled.","The AWS RAM service-linked role exists."],"required_states":["AWS Organizations all features enabled","AWS RAM organization sharing enabled through AWS RAM","AWS RAM service-linked role exists","Resource type supports the chosen principal type"]}
explanation: The statement contains two alternatives, but its metadata applies Organizations all-features, AWS RAM organization sharing, and the service-linked-role prerequisites to both. AWS explicitly says individual-account sharing works without enabling Organizations sharing; those prerequisites apply when the principal is an organization or OU.
required_action: Represent the prerequisites conditionally, or split individual-account sharing and organization/OU sharing into separate atomic claims.
suggested_revision: Separate the individual-account path from the OU path so only the OU path requires Organizations sharing, all features, and the RAM service-linked role.

supporting_source.locator: Individual-account sharing versus organization/OU sharing prerequisites
supporting_source.title: Getting started with AWS RAM
